description: Regression scenarios covering the conditional Ubiquitous Language update hook (Step 0.5) and its --skip-ubiquitous-update flag propagation contract.

evals.name: direct-invocation-with-ul-doc
expected_behavior: Step 0 parses $ARGUMENTS and finds no --skip-ubiquitous-update flag.
Step 0.5 runs `test -f docs/ubiquitous-language.md` → exists → invokes `ywc-ubiquitous-language --mode update`.
Any resulting changes to docs/ubiquitous-language.md are classified as IN in Step 2 (session-relevant, just modified).
Step 3 groups domain-code changes and the UL diff into the same logical commit when they reflect the same new term, or splits them when independent.
Final commit message reflects the project's git log style; UL update is not announced in the commit body unless it carries an independent semantic change.
anti_behavior: Asking the user whether to update the UL document — the conditional auto-ON contract says the answer is yes when the file exists.
Creating the UL document if it does not exist — that is the job of `ywc-ubiquitous-language --mode new`, not ywc-commit.

evals.name: delegated-invocation-with-skip-flag
evals.prompt: ywc-create-pr Step 4 에서 ywc-commit 을 --skip-ubiquitous-update flag 와 함께 호출하는 시나리오. ywc-create-pr 의 Step 0.5 가 이미 UL update 를 수행한 직후이며, 같은 PR 생성 흐름에서 ywc-commit 이 호출됨.

preconditions.docs/ubiquitous-language.md: exists, already updated by upstream caller in the same session

expected_behavior: Step 0 parses $ARGUMENTS and stores the --skip-ubiquitous-update flag.
Step 0.5 is skipped entirely. `ywc-ubiquitous-language` is NOT invoked.
Step 2 still classifies the already-modified docs/ubiquitous-language.md as IN (session-relevant), since the upstream caller modified it in the same session.
Final commit set includes the UL diff that was produced upstream — without ywc-commit re-running the update.
anti_behavior: Running ywc-ubiquitous-language despite the flag (double invocation).
Excluding docs/ubiquitous-language.md from the commit because 'I did not run UL update myself' — the file is still IN because upstream session activity touched it.
Treating --skip-ubiquitous-update as a hint to also skip Step 0.5 logic in subsequent steps (the flag scope is Step 0.5 only).

evals.name: ul-document-absent
expected_behavior: Step 0 parses $ARGUMENTS and finds no flag.
Step 0.5 runs `test -f docs/ubiquitous-language.md` → absent → silent skip.
User is NOT prompted to create a UL document. Step 1 (Assess current state) begins immediately.
Workflow proceeds normally with the file change committed under the project's commit-message conventions.
anti_behavior: Prompting the user 'shall I create docs/ubiquitous-language.md?' — that responsibility belongs to ywc-ubiquitous-language invoked directly by the user, not to ywc-commit.
Logging or warning about the missing UL document — silent skip is the contract; noisy skip degrades the UX of small commits.
Failing the workflow because the file is missing.

notes: These scenarios are documentation-grade regression checks. They describe expected agent behavior in natural language; running them as code-level assertions would require a harness that simulates skill activation, file presence, and sub-skill delegation.

When the skill-creator workflow is run against ywc-commit, treat these scenarios as the qualitative review checklist and add programmatic assertions only where behavior is mechanically verifiable (e.g., presence of `ywc-ubiquitous-language --mode update` invocation in the transcript).
